channel.title: Linux Configurations on Memo
channel.description: Recent content in Linux Configurations on Memo

item.title: Compress initramfs
item.description: Compress initramfs # In some cases, the /boot partition is too small for the initramfs to fit. This makes it impossible to install multiple kernels, so the solution is to change the compression method. Edit /etc/initramfs-tools/initramfs.conf and change the compression method to xz (xz has a higher compression ratio, but slows down the decompression speed). sudo vim /etc/initramfs-tools/initramfs.conf - MODULES=most + MODULES=dep - COMPRESS=lz4 + COMPRESS=xz Install xz-utils and update initramfs.

item.title: Gnome Configurations
Japanese Input
item.description: Japanese Input # This is a memo on how to set up Japanese input using Fcitx (Flexible Input Method Framework) and Mozc. Install # fcitx5 fcitx5 is a successor to fcitx. sudo pacman -S fcitx5-mozc fcitx5-im fcitx5-configtool fcitx sudo pacman -S fcitx-mozc fcitx-im fcitx-configtool Configurations # Add Mozc to the input method in fcitx configuration tool. Then, create ~/.pam_environment. vim ~/.pam_environment + GTK_IM_MODULE DEFAULT=fcitx + QT_IM_MODULE DEFAULT=fcitx + XMODIFIERS DEFAULT=\@im=fcitx + SDL_IM_MODULE DEFAULT=fcitx If this does not work, execute the following command.

item.title: Keymaps
item.description: Keymaps # This is a note on how to assign other keys to keys such as Caps lock. Create Custom Key Mapping # Create a directory to store the keymap. sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/share/kbd/keymaps Use dumpkeys to get the original keymaps and save it in my-keymaps.map. sudo dumpkeys | sudo tee /usr/local/share/kbd/keymaps/my-keymaps.map Modify /usr/local/share/kbd/keymaps/my-keymaps.map to change Caps lock to control. sudo vim /usr/local/share/kbd/keymaps/my-keymaps.map - keycode 58 = Caps_Lock + keycode 58 = Control Create /etc/vconsole.

item.title: L2TP IPsec
item.description: L2TP IPsec # paru -S xl2tpd strongswan networkmanager-l2tp
